chief_complaint: An 80-year-old man presented with left hemiparesis.
history: The patient was found to have right internal carotid artery occlusion. Mechanical thrombectomy was successfully performed. An implantable loop recorder implanted 3 years ago showed no episodes of atrial fibrillation (AF) prior to admission. A paroxysmal episode of AF was detected during hospitalisation but did not recur during the following 6 months. Extensive diagnostic evaluation revealed no alternative sources of embolism.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: ECG: paroxysmal episode of AF detected during hospitalisation. Autopsy: extensive fibrosis of the left atrium and patchy subendocardial transthyretin-type amyloid deposits, consistent with atrial cardiomyopathy.
correct_diagnosis: Atrial cardiomyopathy as a potential embolic source in large vessel occlusion
differentials: Atrial cardiomyopathy as a potential embolic source in large vessel occlusion
correct_ranking: Atrial cardiomyopathy as a potential embolic source in large vessel occlusion
